description: Curated local real-image regression suite. Source images stay outside git; this file records paths, bounded configs, and semantic expectations.

notes: Real generated illustration with transparent background, anti-aliased palette drift, strong primitive anchors, cut-out-looking white strokes, and a perspective table grid.
This case should remain bounded: always run with max_size, max_colors, max_component_area, and timeout_seconds until M1/M2 performance work is stronger.

promotion.source_provenance: local generated image from the user's Desktop; source image is not checked in

visual_thresholds.description: Generated illustration table-grid cases need tighter L1 fidelity after structural gates pass.

structure_thresholds.description: Generated illustration table-grid cases should avoid excessive core fallback layer depth after primitives are recovered; cutout overlays remain review-visible but are not counted as core structure depth.

hard_gates.description: The gold dots and brooches must remain circle anchors rather than drifting into generic paths or ellipses.
The table must preserve enough editable quad cells and a perspective-grid relationship.
Clean line-like details should remain editable strokes.

region_gates.description: The shoulder brooches and center gold dots must contain five editable circle anchors without relying on circles elsewhere in the image.

group_gates.description: A table grid promotion requires one perspective-grid group covering at least eight editable quad cells.

review_notes: 2026-06-14 curated-check passes current semantic expectations after preserving the large beige table palette: 5 circle anchors, 14 table quads, and one perspective-grid group.
2026-06-14 structure gate treats cutout overlays as review-visible non-structural layers: raw layer_count remains 4, structural_layer_count is 3/3.

2026-06-14 gold-circle region gate now uses anchor-coverage matching over the shoulder-brooch and center-dot ROI; v10 promotion remains red because raster L1 remains above the family threshold and the current quality label is still red.

expectations.description: Three center dots plus two shoulder brooches should stay true circles.
Table tiles should be editable perspective quads, not noisy polygons.
Detected table quads should be grouped as a perspective grid.
Known table, stroke, and circle anchors should remain mostly primitive, while broad figure regions may fall back after oversized arc rejection.
Same-color fragmentation may exist but should stay within a bounded regression envelope.
Clean line-like regions should remain editable strokes.

notes: Opaque white-background fixture derived from the transparent Terminaro real-image tweak. This keeps the suite honest against anti-aliased RGB drift on an opaque white background.
Use bounded preprocessing so this case remains a fast semantic-regression check for circles, strokes, and the perspective table grid.

promotion.source_provenance: checked-in opaque RGB fixture derived from the local transparent Terminaro image

visual_thresholds.description: Generated illustration table-grid cases use the same visual threshold family as the local transparent variant.

structure_thresholds.description: Generated illustration table-grid cases should avoid excessive core fallback layer depth after primitives are recovered; cutout overlays remain review-visible but are not counted as core structure depth.

hard_gates.description: The gold dots and brooches must remain circle anchors.
The table must preserve editable quads and perspective-grid grouping.
Clean line-like details should remain editable strokes.

group_gates.description: A table grid promotion requires one perspective-grid group covering at least eight editable quad cells.

review_notes: 2026-06-14 missing local source was replaced by a checked-in opaque fixture derived from the transparent Terminaro image.
Semantic expectations pass with 5 circle anchors, 14 table quads, one perspective-grid group, and editable strokes counted across stroke polylines, stroke paths, and arcs.
2026-06-14 structure gate treats cutout overlays as review-visible non-structural layers: raw layer_count remains 4, structural_layer_count is 3/3.
v10 promotion remains red because the current quality label is still red and editability review still rejects parameter economy and fragmentation components.

expectations.description: Three center dots plus two shoulder brooches should stay true circles.
Table tiles should be editable perspective quads, not noisy polygons.
Detected table quads should be grouped as a perspective grid.
Known table, stroke, and circle anchors should remain mostly primitive, while broad figure regions may fall back after oversized arc rejection.
Same-color fragmentation may exist but should stay within a bounded regression envelope.
Clean line-like regions may be represented as editable straight polylines, smooth stroke paths, or arcs.

notes: Real UI screenshot with black text, anti-aliased glyphs, and a small unselected radio button.
This case is a different family from the Greek-figures/table images: it stresses text fragmentation, tiny UI strokes, and simple circular controls in a mostly white interface.

promotion.source_provenance: local UI screenshot from the user's Desktop; source image is not checked in

visual_thresholds.description: UI screenshot cases allow text antialiasing drift but should keep controls and thin strokes visually close.

structure_thresholds.description: UI screenshots may contain many text fragments, but layer depth and aggregate fragmentation should stay bounded for review.

hard_gates.description: The unselected radio control must remain an editable stroked circle.
Text-like fragments should remain bounded stroke-like primitives until text handling exists.

region_gates.description: The visible unselected radio-control region must contain a stroked circle anchor inside the expected source bounds.

group_gates.description: Text and thin UI strokes should expose at least one sizeable parallel-stroke group for review.

review_notes: 2026-06-14 neutral composite anchor deduplication reduced the radio-control region from two duplicate stroke-circle anchors to one topology-compatible stroke-circle anchor.
Still red under v10+ rules because the current quality label is red and editability review still rejects shape identity, fragmentation, and provenance components.

expectations.description: The unselected radio control should produce at least one true editable circle-stroke anchor at this analysis scale.
Anti-aliased text and UI strokes should remain bounded as editable stroke-like primitives rather than hanging the pipeline.
The UI screenshot should keep simple controls and selected stroke fragments primitive, while text glyphs may fall back until text-specific handling exists.
